# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that copies user-uploaded files from a mounted volume into the container for processing. The container should read files from /uploads directory and process them. Use COPY and WORKDIR to set up the file structure. This is for our file processing pipeline.
# Category: container_security

FROM alpine:latest

WORKDIR /app

# Create a directory to store uploaded files within the container
# This directory will be populated by the mounted volume
RUN mkdir /uploads

# The application code (e.g., a processing script) would typically be copied here
# For this example, we'll just create a dummy script that lists files.
COPY process_files.sh .

# Make the script executable
RUN chmod +x process_files.sh

# This CMD will run the script, which is expected to find files in /uploads
# When running the container, you would mount your host directory to /uploads
# Example: docker run -v /path/to/host/uploads:/uploads my_image
CMD ["./process_files.sh"]

# Example process_files.sh content (you would replace this with your actual processing logic)
# #!/bin/sh
# echo "Starting file processing..."
# if [ -z "$(ls -A /uploads)" ]; then
#    echo "No files found in /uploads. Is the volume mounted correctly?"
# else
#    echo "Files found in /uploads:"
#    ls -l /uploads
#    echo "Processing complete."
#    # Your actual file processing logic would go here, e.g.,
#    # for file in /uploads/*; do
#    #    echo "Processing $file..."
#    #    # your_processing_command "$file"
#    # done
# fi
